data.title: European markets rise; UK market falls on disappointing retail sales data
data.description:
Update: This article reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) -0.20% at 8,775.

UK retail sales fall sharply in May.  

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.27% at 23,351.

France (CAC:IND)  +0.48% at 7,590.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.13% at 563.5 on Friday. Investors kept a close eye on the conflict in the Middle East between Iran and Israel, and potential direct U.S. involvement.

Eutelsat (OTCPK:EUTLF) shares rose after the European satellite operator announced a 1.35 billion euro ($1.55 billion) capital raise backed by the French state and other shareholders.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 2 basis points at 4.37%.

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.51%.

The UK's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points at 4.53%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.

data.title: Five Point to acquire controlling interest in business, operations of Hearthstone
data.description:
Five Point Holdings (NYSE:FPH) announced on Friday the acquisition of a controlling interest in the business and operations of the capital solutions provider to homebuilders, Hearthstone.

The California-based mixed-use planned communities developer has entered into a definitive agreement for the acquisition of 75% of a newly formed entity, Hearthstone Residential Holdings, that will include substantially all of the business and operations of Hearthstone.

The remaining 25% is set to be retained by entities affiliated with Hearthstone CEO Mark Porath. Hearthstone will continue to be led by its existing management team.

The terms of the acquisition, expected to close by the end of the third quarter, were not disclosed.

data.title: Cybercriminals have gained access to about 16B login credentials - report
data.description:
Cybercriminals have gained access to about 16B login credentials, according to a report by Cybernews, in what they claim to be the biggest ever data breach.

Vilius Petkauskas, the deputy editor at the news outlet, wrote, "Our team has been closely monitoring the web since the beginning of the year. So far, they’ve discovered 30 exposed datasets containing from tens of millions to over 3.5 billion records each. In total, the researchers uncovered an unimaginable 16 billion records."

"From social media and corporate platforms to VPNs and developer portals, no stone was left unturned," the report said.

According to the report, all of the datasets were exposed only briefly, long enough for Cybernews researchers to uncover them, but not long enough to find who was controlling the data.

They claim that most of the data in the leaked datasets is a mix of details from stealer malware, credential stuffing sets, and repackaged leaks. It included some information from cloud services, business-oriented data, and even locked files. 

"This is not just a leak—it’s a blueprint for mass exploitation," researchers at Cybernews said. "What’s especially concerning is the structure and recency of these datasets—these aren’t just old breaches being recycled. This is fresh, weaponizable intelligence at scale."

The report said "it’s impossible" to know how many people or accounts were actually exposed due to overlapping records. Most of the information included a URL, followed by login details and a password, which Cybernews claims is how most modern infostealers collect data. Some dataset names indicated that malware was used to collect the data.

Cybernews researchers said a leak of this magnitude would serve as fuel for phishing campaigns, account takeovers, ransomware intrusions, and business email compromise attacks.

The report said it’s "virtually guaranteed" that some of the leaked datasets were owned by cybercriminals.

data.title: Sarepta downgraded at William Blair on near-term upside potential
data.description:
• William Blair downgraded Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) to market perform from outperform saying the company has limited near-term upside as a result of a decline in its peak revenue opportunity.
  • Analyst Sami Corwin noted a "growing number of uncertain variables will be a deterrent for investors."
  • She mentioned that the recent second case of fatal acute liver failure in an Elevidys (delandistrogene movaparvovec) patient is a hangover for the stock.
  • Corwin added that investors are still concerned that the company has been unclear about the number of additional non-ambulatory patients that may have been treated and could also develop liver failure. As a result, there is "uncertainty over the risk of another fatality in the near term."

data.title: Fed reiterates cautious rate stance in wake of economic uncertainty
data.description:
The Federal Reserve reiterated its wait-and-see approach to interest rates, saying its "current stance of monetary policy leaves it well positioned to wait for more clarity on the outlook for inflation and economic activity and respond in a timely way to potential economic developments," according to its monetary policy report published on Friday.

Since the start of the year, the Federal Open Market Committee has kept the target range for the policy rate at 4.25%-4.50%, with the labor market at or near full employment and inflation moderating toward 2%. And the Fed is still implementing its plan to significantly lower the size of its balance sheet, albeit at a slower pace, through its quantitative tightening program.

On the broader economy, the central bank pointed to a still-solid labor market, with a low jobless rate and moderate pace of job gains so far this year, as well as a continued moderation in inflation, which still remains above target. 

And, while real gross domestic product growth stalled in Q1 2025, there was still moderate growth in private domestic final demand, "reflecting a modest increase in consumer spending and a jump in capital spending," the report said.

On financial stability, the Fed gave a thumbs-up, calling the financial system "resilient" even during April's bout of market volatility induced by President Donald Trump's tariff announcements. 

"The banking system remained sound and resilient, with continued increases in regulatory capital, while outside the banking sector, leverage at hedge funds remained near historically high levels," the Fed noted.

The Fed said it plans to conclude its review of its monetary policy framework by late summer, at which point the outcomes will be published. The review covered topics tied to the jobs market, inflation dynamics and economic uncertainty.

data.title: Capricor falls after long-term trial data for Duchenne therapy
data.description:
Capricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CAPR) lost ~15% on Friday after the cell therapy developer posted long-term data from an open-label extension trial for its lead asset, Deramiocel, which is currently under FDA priority review for a rare muscle disorder called Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

Patients on Deramiocel in its HOPE-2 PLE study witnessed a median change of -0.5 points compared to baseline over four years, the San Diego, California-based biotech said without specifying the measure.

Additionally, the performance of the upper limb indicated an average decline of 0.6 points in the fourth year among deramiocel-treated patients compared to 1.8 points in the first year, the company said, implying that its experimental drug can slow skeletal muscle disease progression.

The cell therapy continued to show a favorable tolerability profile over the entire duration of the study.

However, following the readout, Capricor (NASDAQ:CAPR) shares plunged in a move attributed to the abrupt departure of the director and deputy director of the cell and gene therapy division of the FDA’s Center for Biologics Research and Evaluation (CBER).

Nicole Verdun, FDA’s Super Office Director of Therapeutic Products, and her deputy Rachael Anatol were forced out of the agency, multiple media outlets reported on Wednesday.

However, Oppenheimer analyst Leland Gershell noted that their departures shouldn’t affect the ongoing review of Deramiocel at the CBER, which recently scheduled an AdCom meeting on July 30 to discuss the company’s marketing application.

“The company continues to prepare for the anticipated advisory committee meeting, for which timing is presumably July 31, pending confirmation or change,” Bloomberg News reported, quoting Gershell. Capricor (NASDAQ:CAPR) was not immediately available for Seeking Alpha’s requests for comments.

data.title: Darden Restaurants looks to sell Bahama Breeze chain, beats FQ4 expectations
data.description:
Shares of Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) have reversed early losses and are back in positive territory following the release of better-than-expected fiscal fourth quarter results, an increased quarterly dividend, along with plans to sell its struggling Bahama Breeze chain.

Last month, the company announced the closure of 15 Bahama Breeze restaurants to “allow for the focus on highest performing restaurants and strengthen the brand’s overall performance.” In 2024, the chain reported a 7.7% decline in sales from the year prior and projects sales to decline another 4.6% in 2025.

Given the downbeat outlook for the chain, CEO Rick Cardenas said Darden (NYSE:DRI) is considering “strategic alternatives” for the Bahama Breeze brand, including a potential sale or converting existing restaurants into other Darden brands.

“After careful review, we’ve determined [the chain] is no longer a strategic priority and believe Bahama Breeze has the potential to benefit from a new owner,” Cardenas said on the company’s earnings call with analysts.

For the reported quarter, Darden (NYSE:DRI) earned a better-than-expected profit of $2.98 per share, up 12.5% from last year. Thanks to a 4.6% increase in same-restaurant sales and 103 new Chuy’s Tex Mex restaurants, total sales increased 10.6% to $3.3B, $40M above expectations.

Looking ahead to FY26, while the closure of one-third Bahama Breeze restaurants along with several Season 15 and Eddie V’s locations is expected to be a headwind to FY26 sales growth, the company anticipates revenue to increase by 7% to 8%, translating into sales of $12.95B to $13.06B, missing $13.14B estimate.

Earnings per share are pegged for $10.50 to $10.70 versus $10.75 estimates.  

Darden (DRI) also expects comparable sales growth of +2% to +3.5% from +1.5% in 2025, and to open 60-65 new restaurants.

Finally, the company lifted its dividend by 7.1% to $1.50 per share, payable on August 1 to shareholders of record as of July 10.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow are mixed as Fed official hints at a potential rate cut in July
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages are mixed on Friday as Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller hinted at potential rate cuts in July.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finds itself near even, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) is up 0.3%, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has drifted lower by 0.2%.

Across the S&P 500, eight of the 11 sectors are trading in the green, with Utilities and Energy posting the strongest gains while Communication Services has been the weakest performer so far.

U.S. Treasury yields have edged lower, with the shorter-end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) sliding 2 basis points to 3.91% while the longer-end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) is flat at 4.39%.

Today also marks the second triple witching event of the year—a day on which $6.5T worth of contracts tied to stock options, stock index futures and stock index options will expire. Such days have typically seen volatility in equity markets.

Waller said Friday he sees little risk that tariffs will stoke inflation and signaled the central bank could start cutting interest rates as soon as July.

On the geopolitical front President Donald Trump announced that he will decide within the next two weeks if the U.S. should launch an attack on Iran, pressuring Tehran to the negotiating table for a nuclear deal.

"Markets are recovering a bit this morning after White House spokeswoman Leavitt last night said that Trump had dictated a message saying that 'based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks,'" Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

That means two weeks of uncertainty for financial markets, but investors are still inclined to see the Middle East conflict as a local, not a global, economic issue, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

From an economic vantage point, the Philly Fed Manufacturing gauge stalled at -4 in June, missing consensus. Furthermore, the U.S. Leading Indicator figures inched down 0.1% to 99.0 in May, matching the consensus estimates.

data.title: Weight-loss drugs should be among primary options to prevent heart disease: cardiology experts
data.description:
Shares of weight-loss drugmakers Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) spiked after the American College of Cardiology (ACC) recommended that obesity drugs should be among the primary options when selecting treatments to prevent heart disease.

In its newest Concise Clinical Guidance issued on Friday, the nonprofit medical association noted that obesity-linked heart diseases such as hypertension "can increasingly be addressed with modern obesity medications with fewer risks than procedure-based interventions."

ACC's latest guidelines contrasted with its previous recommendation of prioritizing lifestyle modifications before using weight loss medications, in a move that could potentially widen healthcare coverage for the GLP-1 blockbusters.

Listed health insurers: UnitedHealth (UNH), Humana (HUM), CVS Health (CVS), Cigna (CI), Clover Health (CLOV), Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), Centene (CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), and Elevance Health (ELV). 

Novo Nordisk (NVO) has already received FDA approval for its weight loss therapy, Wegovy for the reduction of major adverse cardiovascular events such as heart attacks in obese or overweight people with established cardiovascular disease.

In August, Lilly (NYSE:LLY) said its rival therapy, tirzepatide, marketed as Zepbound for weight loss, cut the risk of heart failure outcomes with a statistically significant effect in a Phase 3 trial for adults with heart failure and obesity. 

"Patients should not be required to 'try and fail' lifestyle changes prior to initiating pharmacotherapy," ACC's heart disease experts, led by Olivia Gilbert, a cardiologist at Atrium Health Wake Forest Baptist Medical Center, wrote in the CGC report. 

However, the ACC cautioned against abandoning lifestyle modifications altogether. "Lifestyle interventions should always be offered in conjunction with obesity medications," Gilbert added. 

"Weight management by the cardiovascular community needs to be embraced, given both the prevalence of obesity and the impact it has on many forms of [cardiovascular disease]."

data.title: SEC Chair Atkins axes proposed market rules from predecessor Gensler
data.description:
• SEC Chairman Paul Atkins has withdrawn a litany of proposed market rules that were never finalized by his predecessor, Gary Gensler.
  • The SEC's Rulemaking Activity page lists 14 rules that were withdrawn on June 12. They include areas such as cybersecurity risk management for investment advisors; conflicts of interest associated with the use of predictive data analytics; disclosures about environmental, social, and governance investment practices; safeguarding advisory client assets; using artificial intelligence to offer guidance to investors; and cryptocurrency exchanges.
  • "As the chairman has said, we are getting back to our roots of promoting, rather than stifling, innovation," the SEC told the Financial Times. "The markets innovate, and the SEC should not be in the business of telling them to stand still."
  • Atkins' "desire seems to have been to junk Gensler's whole approach to the markets," Columbia Law School Professor John Coffee told the newspaper.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
